title: made.json
description: A decentralized feed of made things published at /made.json on a domain. Spec v1.0.

version.description: Spec version this file conforms to.

self.description: Canonical URL of this made.json. Lets aggregators detect mirrors and follow renames.

author.description: Default author applied to items that don't override it.

items.description: The made things published in this feed. May be empty.

id.description: Stable identifier across renames. Unique within this file. Lowercase URL-safe slug recommended. Aggregators fall back to url when absent.

kind.description: Artifact kind hint. Recommended values include app, tool, skill, prompt, workflow, agent, template, mcp-server, cli, library, and extension. Readers MUST tolerate unknown values.

url.description: Primary launch surface. Typically a web URL, but may point to any installable target.

version.description: Item's own version string. Free-form; semver recommended.

targets.description: Other ways to install or launch this item. Readers MUST ignore unknown 'kind' values without erroring.

vibe_coded.description: Self-attestation that this item was primarily AI-assisted.

prompt_log.description: Public prompt log or transcript URL (e.g., a Claude share link, a gist, a GitHub repo path).

forkable.description: Author invites forks. SHOULD imply 'source' is published.

source.description: URL to the source repository or canonical source location.

replaces.description: URI of the upstream item this forks. Either a regular https URL or an app:// URI of the form app://<host>/<id>.

kind.description: Platform target. The list below is a hint, not a closed enum — readers MUST tolerate unknown kinds.

command.description: For 'cli' or 'prompt' kinds: the literal command line or prompt body.

label.description: Human-readable label, e.g., 'Install on Mac' or 'Open in Claude Code'.

checksum.description: Optional integrity hash for downloadable targets. Recommended for binaries.

url.description: Author home page. Used as the verification anchor; social profiles SHOULD link back here.

social.description: Verified social handles. A verifying agent fetches each entry's URL and looks for a backlink to author.url or to the made.json host. Verification is the agent's responsibility, not the file's.
